target.name: Hugging Face
target.founder: Clément Delangue, Julien Chaumond, Thomas Wolf

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $4.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Wayve
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $8.6B

private.name: Cohere
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $7B

private.name: Hive AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $2B

private.name: Labelbox
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series D), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Hugging Face is a Series D-stage AI & Software company seeking valuation assessment against a comp universe of 1 public peer (Palantir) and 8 private companies in the same sector. The target raised $400M to achieve a $4.5B last post-money valuation.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical Series D-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.5x applied to the $4.5B base, yields $6.8B. Peer-median analysis across 4 same-stage, same-sector comparables—Wayve ($8.6B), Cohere ($7.0B), Hive AI ($2.0B), and Labelbox ($1.0B)—produces a median of $4.5B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $8.6B. The dispersion among peers reflects material variance in business scale and stage maturity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $3.8B (low) to $7.8B (high), with a median of $5.6B. This implies modest step-up from the current $4.5B post-money valuation. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing event, the sustainability of user adoption and revenue runway relative to higher-valued peers, and the relevance of public-market comparables given AI & Software sector repricing. The peer set dispersion—a 8.6x gap between min and max—suggests stage and business-model heterogeneity warrant scenario-based modeling tied to specific growth and profitability milestones.
